_note: Outdoor feed: ski Dec-Apr, backpack May-Oct. Western US focus. Refreshed every ~3 weeks by GHA cron.

items.headline: Skier Shares 2026 Experience Of Hiking Up & Skiing Down Tuckerman Ravine
items.snark: Tuckerman Ravine mid-April is the East Coast proving ground and this POV captures the whole grind: bootpack up Headwall Right, the steep glassy descent, the earned glory.

items.headline: New Film Captures The Allure & Perils Of Skiing Tuckerman Ravine
items.snark: The Proving Ground just dropped and it's frank about what Tuck's demands. Avalanche forecasts, self-arrest drills, and the reality that not everyone comes home clean. Essential viewing.

items.headline: New Hampshire Skiers Nearly Hit By Spring Avalanche
items.snark: April 9 in Tuckerman Chute and a skier-triggered slab nearly caught multiple people at the ravine floor. Spring corn doesn't mean zero avy hazard. Stay sharp.

items.headline: Snow Report: Spring Corn and High-Elevation Sun
items.snark: Mt. Bachelor bagged nearly a foot early April and now it's dialing in that perfect freeze-thaw window. Morning firm, late morning butter, and Sunrise Lodge views that don't quit.

items.headline: Spring Came Early: Corn Skiing on Mt. Shasta
items.snark: Temps 20 degrees above average and Shasta corn season started ahead of schedule. Avalanche Gulch above 10k is silky by mid-morning if you time the window right. Big volcano laps.

items.headline: REVIEW: Voile's Newest Ski Completes Telemark's Modern Touring Paradigm
items.snark: Voile Vector ACE is the paulownia-carbon tele ski that tours light and still holds an edge on frozen late-season crust. For the free-heel crowd hunting big spring objectives.

items.headline: Arapahoe Basin Ski Area to offer full access to 2025-26 Ikon Pass members, starting this spring
items.snark: A-Basin went full unlimited on the Ikon Pass starting March 31. No blackouts, no caps. The Beach at 13k and corn laps into June just became a whole lot more crowded.

items.headline: Hiking, Backpacking, & Exploring Enchanted New Mexico
items.snark: Pecos Wilderness opens late May and Santa Barbara Divide is pure ridge-walk glory. Thirteen-thousand-foot peaks, alpine lakes, bighorn sheep, and almost nobody out there. Taos backyard gold.

items.headline: 4 Great Backpacking Trips in New Mexico
items.snark: Jordan Hot Springs in the Gila requires 15 river crossings waist-deep depending on runoff and the reward is a 94-degree soak in the canyon. Sandals, trekking poles, and patience required.

items.headline: A Short Guide to Hiking the Colorado Trail
items.snark: CT thru-hike window is July 1 to September 30 but if you're planning now you can dial in resupply, monsoon strategies, and whether you're really ready for 567 miles and 90k vertical.
